tasks.title: Fix GitHub (GITrepo) connector scope — grant access to brad-claude-context + monarch-dashboard
tasks.notes:
SYMPTOM (verified 2026-07-17): via the GITrepo (GitHub) Claude connector, Claude can read poserpilot/brad-portal (public) but gets 404 on BOTH poserpilot/brad-claude-context and poserpilot/monarch-dashboard. 404 = the token/app backing the connector does not have those private repos in scope. The connector itself is connected + enabled in chat; the "to do" portal connector works fine. So this is a repo-ACCESS scope gap, not a dead connector. Effect: Claude can't read your canonical context repo or open the WORKING_DOC_IDS change as a PR (T-0012 is a manual PC edit until this is fixed).

FIX — pick the branch that matches how GITrepo is authed:

A) If GITrepo uses a fine-grained Personal Access Token:
  1. GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Fine-grained tokens → open the token used by the connector.
  2. Repository access → "Only select repositories" → add brad-claude-context AND monarch-dashboard (or switch to "All repositories").
  3. Permissions: Contents = Read and write; Pull requests = Read and write; Metadata = Read.
  4. Save (regenerate if required). If the token value changed, update it in Claude → Settings → Connectors → GITrepo → reconnect.

B) If GITrepo is the GitHub App connector:
  1. GitHub → Settings → Applications → Installed GitHub Apps → open the app for this connector → Configure.
  2. Repository access → add brad-claude-context + monarch-dashboard (or "All repositories") → Save.
  3. Claude → Settings → Connectors → GITrepo → reconnect/refresh if it doesn't pick up immediately.

C) If you actually built a SEPARATE custom "context" connector yesterday:
  It is NOT appearing as connected/enabled in this workspace (only GITrepo + "to do" show). Re-add it: Claude → Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector → its /mcp URL → authorize → make sure it's toggled ON for the chat.

VERIFY: In a new Claude message ask it to read the root of poserpilot/brad-claude-context. Success = it lists the repo tree instead of 404. Then Claude can work from canonical context and open the T-0012 cron change as a PR (your "human-approved via pull request" doctrine) instead of a manual edit.

tasks.title: GitHub connector — exact connection link + PAT steps (companion to T-0013)
tasks.notes:
Concrete connection details for fixing GitHub context access (verified 2026-07-17 against GitHub's official Claude install guide + Claude Help Center). Note: the PAT is entered in YOUR Claude connector settings and stored there — Claude never sees it. GITrepo is ALREADY connected, so widening its token is usually enough; re-adding is only if you prefer a fresh connector.

FASTEST — widen the existing GITrepo token (recommended, no re-add):
Open the PAT that GITrepo uses and grant it the two private repos.
- Fine-grained token: https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens
    Repository access → add brad-claude-context + monarch-dashboard (or "All repositories").
    Permissions → Contents: Read and write · Pull requests: Read and write · Metadata: Read.
- Classic token: https://github.com/settings/tokens → check the "repo" scope (covers all private repos).
If the token value changes, paste it back in Claude → Settings → Connectors → GITrepo.

FRESH RE-ADD — the custom-connector "link":
Claude → Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector.
  Server URL:  https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp   (GitHub's official remote MCP server)
  Auth: PAT with "repo" scope (GitHub's remote server expects a PAT; OAuth-app auth isn't supported there).
If you do this, remove the old GITrepo afterward to avoid duplicate tools.

SCOPE TIGHT: limit the token to brad-claude-context, monarch-dashboard, brad-portal — not "all repositories" — since the token can read/write everything in scope.

VERIFY: ask Claude to read the root of poserpilot/brad-claude-context. Success = repo tree instead of 404. Then Claude can reopen the T-0012 WORKING_DOC_IDS change as a proper PR from canonical context.

Sources: github.com/github/github-mcp-server/blob/main/docs/installation-guides/install-claude.md ; support.claude.com/en/articles/11175166-get-started-with-custom-connectors-using-remote-mcp
